note: We both need to touch exif_transpose in the same two files, so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Proposal: serialize instead of parallelize. I'll add my strip_metadata param now (small, additive change to the function signature/docstring/tests) and send DONE shortly. Please hold off editing ImageOps.py/test_imageops.py until you see my DONE, then add your transform_callback param on top of my changes. That avoids us clobbering each other's edits to the same function. OK?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished strip_metadata param on exif_transpose. Added new kw-only param (default False, backward compatible). When True, after the existing orientation-only logic runs, I clear exif_image.info={} and exif_image.getexif().clear() on the resulting image (covers in_place=True/False and both the transpose-needed and no-transpose branches). Restructured the trailing 'elif not in_place: return image.copy()' into an else-block so strip_metadata is applied uniformly. You're clear to layer transform_callback on top now -- should be additive, no signature/logic conflicts expected since I only touch the tail of the function body after the orientation-stripping block.
